title: Weekly POC check-in — experiment results + guardrails (Northbridge)
preview: Northbridge walked through their first 48 hours of route-level experiment traffic. They are seeing expected cost reductions in variant B but are confused by several Console dashboard behaviors (time window alignment, baseline comparison, and what triggers a guardrail badge). Redwood clarified current semantics (soft-stop defaults, guardrail evaluation windows, known propagation lag and attribution drop issues) and agreed on changes: clearer UI copy/tooltips for deltas, making the evaluation window explicit, and tightening guidance around stickiness keys and streaming retry attribution. Next steps include Redwood validating attribution integrity for their route, suggesting tuned thresholds for p99 latency and error rate, and a short enablement note on how to interpret variant headers and results freshness.

title: POC scoping - route experiments / A-B testing (Northbridge)
preview: Northbridge is scoping a POC to run route-level A/B tests between model variants (cost vs latency vs output quality) and wants first-class measurement in Redwood Console: p50/p95/p99 latency, error rate, throughput, and cost per request / cost per 1k tokens broken out by variant. They need deterministic assignment (stickiness) keyed off their end-user or account id, plus the ability to ramp traffic safely with guardrails (soft-stop initially ok, but they want an option for auto-stop for severe p99 regressions). They also raised enterprise requirements: RBAC on who can start/stop experiments, audit logging of config changes, and clarity on how experiment attribution shows up in logs/headers for debugging. Timeline: start POC in early March, run for ~2 weeks, decision by late March for an April/May rollout on a subset of routes.
